claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 17 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 17 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by gathering some information about your situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I'll need to ask you some questions. Child Benefit eligibility is determined on a per-child basis, so I'll need to understand your situation for each child you're inquiring about.

Let me start with the first question: **Do you have a child or children that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you tell me how many children you'd like to check eligibility for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child I'd like to check eligibility for. Their name is Alex, and they're 17 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. So you have one child, Alex, who is 17 years old.

Since Alex is 17 (which falls in the 16-19 age range), I need to ask about their education or training status.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** This typically includes:
- Full-time non-advanced education (like A-levels, Scottish Highers, or equivalent)
- Approved training programs (such as certain vocational training or apprenticeships)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is currently studying for their A-levels at sixth form college.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful - A-levels at sixth form college is approved non-advanced education. Now I need to check a few more things:

**Is Alex working at all?** If yes, how many hours per week?

(I need to ensure they're not in full-time paid work, which would affect eligibility)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't working at all. They're just focused on their studies at the moment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Good. Now, **is Alex receiving any benefits in their own right?** For example, Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't receive any benefits in their own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect. Just to confirm - **Alex is not in any type of apprenticeship, correct?** They're doing A-levels at sixth form college?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's correct - Alex is just doing A-levels at sixth form college, not an apprenticeship.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Now I need to check your circumstances as the claimant. 

**Do you normally live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great. Now I need to understand your relationship with Alex and your responsibility for them.

**Do you live with Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect. Now, **is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?** For example, another parent or guardian?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to check Alex's living situation. **Is Alex currently:**
- In hospital?
- In local authority care?
- Being fostered?
- Or living normally at home with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is living normally at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent. One final question: **Is the local council paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance costs?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, the council isn't paying anything towards Alex's costs.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent news! Based on all the information you've provided, **you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's a summary of why Alex qualifies:
- **Age**: Alex is 17 years old
- **Education**: Alex is studying A-levels at sixth form college, which is approved non-advanced education
- **Work status**: Alex is not working (under the 24 hours per week threshold)
- **Benefits**: Alex is not receiving benefits in their own right
- **Apprenticeship**: Alex is not in an apprenticeship
- **Your residency**: You live in the UK (Manchester)
- **Responsibility**: You live with Alex and are responsible for them
- **Other claimants**: Nobody else is claiming for Alex
- **Living situation**: Alex lives at home with you normally
- **Council support**: The council is not paying towards Alex's costs

**Next steps to claim:**
- You can apply online or by post using form CH2
- You'll need to provide proof of identity for yourself and Alex
- You'll need Alex's birth certificate or adoption certificate
- Be aware: If you or your partner earns over £60,000, the High Income Child Benefit Charge may apply
- Payments are typically made every 4 weeks

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit or the application process?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's brilliant, thank you so much for all your help! You've explained everything really clearly and I now know that I'm eligible and what I need to do next. I'll get started on the application.
